meta.summary: BandageNG 2026.4.1 for interactive de novo assembly graph visualization through a browser-accessible noVNC session, with headless graph CLI commands included.
meta.description: BandageNG 2026.4.1 for interactive de novo assembly graph visualization through a browser-accessible noVNC session, with headless graph CLI commands included.

test: BandageNG --help | grep -F 'BandageNG' >/dev/null && BandageNG info --help | grep -F 'Display information about a graph' >/dev/null && bandage-ng --help | grep -F 'direct launcher for BandageNG 2026.4.1' >/dev/null && bandage-ng-gui --help | grep -F 'browser-accessible noVNC session' >/dev/null
bandage-ng 2>&1 | grep -F 'no X DISPLAY is available' >/dev/null

makeblastdb -version 2>&1 | grep -F 'makeblastdb' >/dev/null && blastn -version 2>&1 | grep -F 'blastn' >/dev/null && tblastn -version 2>&1 | grep -F 'tblastn' >/dev/null && minimap2 --version >/dev/null && nhmmer -h 2>&1 | grep -F 'HMMER' >/dev/null && hmmsearch -h 2>&1 | grep -F 'HMMER' >/dev/null

mkdir -p /tmp/taf-bandage-ng-smoke && cd /tmp/taf-bandage-ng-smoke && printf 'H	VN:Z:1.0
S	1	ACGTACGT
S	2	TTTT
L	1	+	2	+	0M
' > tiny.gfa && bandage-ng info tiny.gfa | grep -F 'Node count:' >/dev/null && bandage-ng image tiny.gfa tiny.svg >/tmp/taf-bandage-ng-image.log 2>&1 && test -s tiny.svg && grep -F '<svg' tiny.svg >/dev/null

bandage-ng-gui --help >/dev/null && (bandage-ng-gui --geometry 800x600 >/tmp/taf-bandage-ng-gui.log 2>&1 & pid=$!; ok=0; i=0; while [ $i -lt 45 ]; do if curl -fs http://127.0.0.1:5801/vnc.html >/dev/null 2>&1; then ok=1; break; fi; i=$((i + 1)); sleep 1; done; kill $pid 2>/dev/null || true; wait $pid >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; test $ok -eq 1 && grep -F 'http://127.0.0.1:5801/vnc.html?autoconnect=1&resize=scale' /tmp/taf-bandage-ng-gui.log >/dev/null && grep -F 'BandageNG GUI is starting.' /tmp/taf-bandage-ng-gui.log >/dev/null)

upstream.name: BandageNG
upstream.citation: BandageNG is a modern fork of Bandage for interactive visualization of de novo assembly graphs. BandageNG upstream currently lists citation as TBD; for the original Bandage publication see Wick et al. 2015, Bioinformatics.
